Class GenericLinkedList<T>

java.lang.Object
dictionary.GenericLinkedList<T>
Type Parameters:
T - the type of elements held in the list (generic)
All Implemented Interfaces:
Iterable<T>
public class GenericLinkedList<T> extends Object implements Iterable<T>
A generic doubly linked list implementation with forward and backward traversal using a ListIterator. Elements can be added and removed from both the head and tail of the list.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description

    Constructor for the class, empty with no elements.

  • Constructor Details

    • GenericLinkedList

      public GenericLinkedList()
      Constructor for the class, empty with no elements.

  • Method Details

    • iterator

      public ListIterator<T> iterator()
      Returns a ListIterator over the elements in the list.
      Specified by:
      iterator in interface Iterable<T>
      Returns:
      a ListIterator starting at the head (beginning)

    • listIterator

      public ListIterator<T> listIterator()
      Returns a ListIterator over the elements in the list. Includes back and forth traversal as well as element removal.
      Returns:
      a ListIterator starting at the head (beginning)

    • isEmpty

      public Boolean isEmpty()
      Checks if the list is empty.
      Returns:
      true if the list is empty, otherwise false

    • size

      public int size()
      Returns the number of elements in the list.
      Returns:
      the number of elements in the list

    • contains

      public Boolean contains(T element)
      Checks if the element received matches any of the elements in the list.
      Parameters:
      element - the element being searched
      Returns:
      true if the element is found, otherwise false

    • toArray

      public Object toArray()
      Converts list into an array in the same order (head to tail).
      Returns:
      an Object array of all elements found in the list

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Removes all elements from the list.

    • get

      public T get(int index)
      Returns the element at a certain index.
      Parameters:
      index - index of element being retrieved
      Returns:
      the element at the param index

    • getFirst

      public T getFirst()
      Returns first element in list (head).
      Returns:
      data stored at head

    • getLast

      public T getLast()
      Returns the last element in the list (tail).
      Returns:
      data stored at tail

    • addFirst

      public void addFirst(T data)
      Inserts the element at the head.
      Parameters:
      data - the element being added to the front of the list

    • addLast

      public void addLast(T data)
      Inserts the element at the tail.
      Parameters:
      data - the element being added to the end of the list.

    • remove

      public T remove(int index) throws IndexOutOfBoundsException
      Removes and returns the element at index passed as a param. If beginning or end, uses removeFirst() or removeLast(). Otherwise, use iterator to find element.
      Parameters:
      index - the index of the element being removed
      Returns:
      the removed element
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if index is out of range (dependent on list size)

    • remove

      public Boolean remove(T element)
      Removes the element passed as a param if present.
      Parameters:
      element - the element being removed
      Returns:
      true if element was removed or false if not

    • removeFirst

      public T removeFirst() throws NoSuchElementException
      Removes and returns the first element in the list (head).
      Returns:
      the element stored at the removed head node
      Throws:
      NoSuchElementException - if list = empty

    • removeLast

      public T removeLast() throws NoSuchElementException
      Removes and returns the last element in the list (tail).
      Returns:
      the element stored at the removed tail node
      Throws:
      NoSuchElementException - if list = empty

    • increment

      public void increment(T element)
      Checks if element passed occurs in the bucket by traversing and comparing using the equals' method. If it does, it increments the count in its respective node.
      Parameters:
      element - the element being incremented

    • getCount

      public int getCount(T element)
      Gets the count data field of the element passed by traversing and comparing using the equals' method. If the element occurs in the bucket, return its count. If not, return -1 to signal element not present in bucket.
      Parameters:
      element - the element's count being retrieved
      Returns:
      the count of the element
